ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: apk-tools in redis:alpine is a LOW-severity hygiene signal—remove it in the Dockerfile if policy bans package managers in production images.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: The violation text explicitly lists apk-tools with version metadata; whether it is unacceptable depends on your written image standard, not on active exploitation.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: After a compromise, an attacker could try to use apk to pull extra tooling; absent code execution the risk is mostly post-exploitation convenience.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Only redis-cart in ms-demo shares this layer with alert 01, so scope stays on one workload until you standardize base images.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: Policy severity is LOW and the issue is adjacent to, but weaker than, the fixable CVE on the same image—queue behind concrete vuln work unless compliance forbids apk outright.
recommended_actions: Add a multi-stage or cleanup step that purges apk-tools before the final layer.
If Alpine must stay, document an approved exception tied to this workload.
Pair with the zlib CVE fix so one rebuild clears multiple findings.
mitigation_strategies: Gate image promotion on policies that fail CI when package managers remain in runtime layers.
